sourceMovie.title: Beating Hearts
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Beating Hearts for the same reason it broke through in 2024: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Gilles Lellouche keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The romance and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Les Misérables
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Beating Hearts clicked for you, Les Misérables (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and crime, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Beating Hearts?
faq.answer: Try Les Misérables, Pride & Prejudice, Atonement—each shares DNA with Beating Hearts while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Beating Hearts?
faq.answer: Beating Hearts (2024) is best described as Romance, Crime, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Beating Hearts worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
